name: Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control Coverage Repair Assessment for June 6, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 6 surveillance assessment requested RTI human biomarker/trial-watch context, TPN-101/ARPA-H healthy-aging activity, DREAM biology, TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch context, and recent-review triangulation before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages missing source-completeness records for PMID 41929344 FTC/TAF DNA methylation biomarker preprint context, NCT04500847 and NCT07210528 RTI Alzheimer/MCI trial-watch registries, ARPA-H/Brown TPN-101 healthy-aging program activity, NCT06691217 TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch context, PMID 42230995 DREAM somatic-mutation biology, and PMID 42219647 recent TE review triangulation. Coverage is not strong because the staged additions are preprint, no-results registry, program-update, review, or mechanistic sources; no source establishes controlled human retrotransposon target engagement tied to function, durable disease outcomes, clone-size control, mutation-burden reduction, or mortality.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current public support map includes ART-AD open-label 3TC biomarker evidence. Repair stages the FTC/TAF medRxiv post hoc DNA methylation biomarker signal and the LINE-AD/SANTA no-results randomized RTI Alzheimer/MCI registries. This is adequate for the current low-confidence biomarker-stage claim but remains far from functional or durable clinical evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The remaining issue is an evidence gap: no controlled aging-relevant human trial has reported direct TE target engagement tied to functional or clinical benefit.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline covers LINE1/lamivudine aged-mouse inflammatory activation, old-mouse 3TC cognition/neuroinflammation, and lamivudine tauopathy mouse disease-model evidence. Repair adds DREAM mouse brain mutation-burden mechanism context but not a drug-like lifespan intervention.
evidence_categories.gap_note: No direct TE-control intervention has shown robust lifespan extension in mammals within the staged public map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repaired map now covers SIRT6 LINE1 repression, senescence LINE1 cDNA/type I interferon activation, NRTI/lamivudine animal evidence, FTC/TAF biomarker and pharmacology contrast, DREAM/DNA-repair mutation-burden biology, and recent TE review triangulation.

The staged records explicitly account for ART-AD size/open-label limits, FTC/TAF preprint and post hoc limits, FTC/TDF null contrast, LINE-AD and SANTA no-results status, TPN-101 program-update-only status, no-results NCT06691217 CHIP trial status, and the distinction between downstream cytokine modulation and direct mutation or clone control.

Safety and durability remain thin as field-level evidence gaps. ART-AD is small and 24 weeks; FTC/TAF is 12-week post hoc biomarker context; LINE-AD, SANTA, NCT06691217, and the TPN-101 healthy-aging program have no posted results; and no source establishes long-term preventive-use safety, resistance-context, direct target engagement, tissue specificity, functional benefit, or durability.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This remains an evidence gap requiring future results, not another immediate coverage-repair pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages LINE-AD NCT04500847, SANTA NCT07210528, and TET2 CHIP canakinumab NCT06691217 as no-results trial-watch records. It also checked TPN-101/censavudine registry searches and found no ClinicalTrials.gov aging-program record during this pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Watch for LINE-AD, SANTA, TET2 CHIP canakinumab, and TPN-101 healthy-aging registry/results updates during ordinary surveillance.

evidence_categories.rationale: Repair stages PMID 42219647 as recent TE aging review triangulation and uses it to clarify TE biomarker-effector framing and intervention-class heterogeneity.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A deeper citation-network audit could improve strong coverage but is not required before ordinary surveillance.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current public baseline includes key LINE1/SIRT6, LINE1/senescence, 3TC mouse, ART-AD, CANTOS/TET2, and TIMI CHIP anchors. Repair stages the missing translational anchors around FTC/TAF, RTI Alzheimer trial-watch records, TPN-101 healthy-aging activity, DREAM, and a recent TE review.

The staged outlook separates ART-AD 3TC, FTC/TAF biological-age biomarker evidence, emtricitabine and FTC/TAF Alzheimer/MCI registries, TPN-101 healthy-aging program activity, DREAM/DNA-repair somatic-mutation biology, TET2 CHIP canakinumab downstream inflammatory modulation, and broad TE review context.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair reviewed the June 1 public outlook and bootstrap artifacts, June 6 surveillance and coverage assessment, PubMed metadata and abstracts for PMID 41929344, PMID 42230995, and PMID 42219647, ClinicalTrials.gov API records for NCT04500847, NCT07210528, and NCT06691217, TPN-101/censavudine ClinicalTrials.gov searches, ARPA-H award 4201, Brown University TPN-101/aging program material, and Transposon Therapeutics sponsor release context.
source_selection_notes: PMID 41929344 was staged as bounded preprint human biomarker context, not as definitive proof of gerotherapeutic effect.
NCT04500847 and NCT07210528 were staged as no-results trial-watch records for the RTI Alzheimer/MCI branch.
ARPA-H award 4201 and Brown program material were staged as activity sources for TPN-101 healthy-aging momentum; the Transposon Therapeutics release was logged as context only.
NCT06691217 was staged as a no-results prospective TET2 CHIP/canakinumab trial-watch record with VAF and vascular-inflammation endpoints.
PMID 42230995 was staged as somatic-mutation mechanism support, not as a human intervention source.
PMID 42219647 was staged as recent TE review triangulation, not as primary efficacy evidence.

known_gaps.description: No staged human source shows controlled RTI or TPN-101 reduction of direct LINE1, retrotransposon cDNA, cGAS-STING, interferon, tissue-inflammatory, or somatic-mutation biomarkers tied to cognition, physical function, frailty, disease outcomes, or mortality.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal until controlled target-engagement and functional or disease-outcome evidence appears.

known_gaps.description: LINE-AD, SANTA, the TET2 CHIP canakinumab vascular-inflammation trial, and the TPN-101 healthy-aging program lack posted results or a complete healthy-aging registry record.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch NCT04500847, NCT07210528, NCT06691217, and TPN-101/ARPA-H registry/publication activity during ordinary surveillance.

known_gaps.description: Short ART-AD and FTC/TAF biomarker evidence plus no-results trial-watch records do not establish long-term preventive-use safety, resistance-context boundaries, tissue specificity, durability, or risk-benefit for RTIs or cytokine blockade in aging populations.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require longer-duration safety, tolerability, tissue target-engagement, and durability data before increasing confidence.

known_gaps.description: No staged human source shows direct reduction of somatic mutation burden, CHIP clone size, TET2 variant allele fraction, LINE1 cDNA, or retrotransposon activity under intervention.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not interpret canakinumab, NRTIs, or DREAM context as direct mutation or clone control unless future studies report clone-dynamics or mutation-burden results.

known_gaps.description: PMID 41929344 is a preprint post hoc DNA methylation analysis in young healthy adults, with no direct TE readouts and reported manufacturer-related conflicts for some authors.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Monitor peer review, correction, replication, and prospective placebo-controlled geroscience studies before treating FTC/TAF as more than low-confidence biomarker support.

known_gaps.description: The track combines LINE1/reverse-transcriptase biology, FTC/TAF pharmacology, TPN-101 development, DREAM/DNA-repair somatic-mutation biology, CHIP clone biology, and downstream cytokine modulation.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future public changes explicit about which branch is being supported and avoid generalizing evidence across NRTIs, TPN-101, DREAM, CHIP, or cytokine blockade.

next_coverage_action: Review the staged coverage-repair bundle through source-fidelity, interpretation/forecast, safety-limitations, and taxonomy-mapping lanes. If approved and published, return transposon and somatic mutation control to ordinary surveillance with special watch on LINE-AD, SANTA, TPN-101/ARPA-H registry activity, FTC/TAF peer review, and NCT06691217 results.
